# Atomic Learning — a compiler for inspectable curricula

> *Every sentence has a receipt. Every claim has a gate.* **Give it an open textbook chapter. It produces an offline learning path where every generated sentence can be challenged against its source.**

## Concepts in prerequisite order

- [Vectors](llms-full.txt#vectors): A vector is a fixed-length array whose elements are scalars.
- [Dot Product](llms-full.txt#dot-product): The dot product of two vectors sums the products of elements at corresponding positions.
- [Matrix–Vector Product](llms-full.txt#matrix-vector-product): A matrix–vector product contains one dot product between the input vector and each row of the matrix.
- [Softmax](llms-full.txt#softmax): Softmax exponentiates scores and normalizes them into values that sum to one.
- [Queries, Keys, and Values](llms-full.txt#qkv): Attention can be framed using a query applied to a collection of key–value pairs.
- [Attention Pooling](llms-full.txt#attention-pooling): Attention pooling combines values linearly using weights determined by query–key compatibility.
- [Self-Attention](llms-full.txt#self-attention): Self-attention lets every token attend to every other token in the same sequence.
- [Positional Encoding](llms-full.txt#positional-encoding): Positional encodings provide token-order information to models as additional token-associated inputs.
- [Softmax Preserves Ordering](llms-full.txt#softmax-ordering): Softmax preserves score ordering, so the largest input score identifies the class with the highest output probability.
- [Vector Norm](llms-full.txt#vector-norm): A vector norm provides a scalar measure of the vector's magnitude.
